The Good Friday Agreement, also known as the Belfast Agreement, was a peace agreement that was signed on April 10, 1998, in Belfast, Northern Ireland. It helped to bring an end to the Troubles, a period of sectarian violence that had plagued the region since the 1960s. The agreement established a power-sharing government in Northern Ireland and set out a framework for peaceful coexistence and reconciliation between the unionist and nationalist communities.
